tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild renal impairment (elevated creatinine), hypoglycemia, and markedly abnormal urinalysis with proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance. HbA1c is unexpectedly low, possibly due to recent hypoglycemia or analytical interference. Findings suggest possible acute or chronic kidney dysfunction, metabolic derangement (possibly diabetes with poor glycemic control), and possible liver involvement. Clinical correlation and further evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
